disclaimer: Prototype content transcribed from public WHO/UNICEF and Ghana Health Service job aids. NOT yet reviewed by a qualified health professional. Must be clinically validated before any real-world use.

items.question: Is there heavy vaginal bleeding?
items.action: Refer immediately to the nearest facility with emergency obstetric care.
items.source: WHO Pregnancy, Childbirth, Postpartum and Newborn Care (IMPAC) — community danger signs in pregnancy

items.question: Has the woman had convulsions or fits?
items.action: Refer immediately — possible eclampsia. Keep her safe from injury while arranging transport.
items.source: WHO IMPAC — danger signs in pregnancy and postpartum

items.question: Does she have a severe headache with blurred vision?
items.source: WHO IMPAC — danger signs in pregnancy

items.question: Does she have a high fever?
items.action: Assess further and refer if fever persists or she appears unwell.
items.source: WHO IMPAC — danger signs in pregnancy and postpartum

items.question: Does she have severe abdominal pain?
items.source: WHO IMPAC — danger signs in pregnancy

items.question: Has fetal movement reduced or stopped?
items.action: Refer for assessment as soon as possible.
items.source: WHO IMPAC — danger signs in pregnancy

items.question: Has there been a sudden gush of fluid from the vagina before labour?
items.action: Refer for assessment — possible premature rupture of membranes.
items.source: WHO IMPAC — danger signs in pregnancy

items.question: Is there foul-smelling vaginal discharge (pregnancy or postpartum)?
items.source: WHO IMPAC — danger signs in pregnancy and postpartum

items.question: Is the newborn unable to breastfeed or drink at all?
items.source: WHO IMCI Chart Booklet — general danger signs

items.question: Does the newborn vomit everything it is fed?
items.source: WHO IMCI Chart Booklet — general danger signs

items.question: Has the newborn had convulsions?
items.source: WHO IMCI Chart Booklet — general danger signs

items.question: Is the newborn lethargic, unconscious, or moving only when stimulated?
items.source: WHO IMCI Chart Booklet — general danger signs, young infant

items.question: Is the newborn breathing very fast or with difficulty?
items.source: WHO IMCI Chart Booklet — young infant danger signs

items.question: Does the newborn have low body weight or feel cold to the touch?
items.action: Keep warm with skin-to-skin contact and refer for assessment.
items.source: WHO IMCI Chart Booklet — low weight and hypothermia guidance

items.question: Is the umbilical stump bleeding, red, or discharging?
items.source: WHO/USAID Postnatal Care Pre-Discharge Checklist

items.question: Is the child unable to drink or breastfeed anything?
items.source: WHO IMCI Chart Booklet — general danger signs

items.question: Does the child vomit everything it is given?
items.source: WHO IMCI Chart Booklet — general danger signs

items.question: Has the child had convulsions during this illness?
items.source: WHO IMCI Chart Booklet — general danger signs

items.question: Is the child unusually sleepy, difficult to wake, or unconscious?
items.source: WHO IMCI Chart Booklet — general danger signs

items.question: Does the child's lower chest wall pull in when breathing?
items.action: Refer immediately — sign of severe pneumonia.
items.source: WHO IMCI Chart Booklet — cough/difficult breathing assessment

items.question: Does the child have diarrhoea with sunken eyes or is unable to drink normally?
items.action: Assess for dehydration and refer if signs worsen.
items.source: WHO IMCI Chart Booklet — diarrhoea assessment

items.question: Does the child show visible severe wasting or swelling of both feet?
items.action: Refer immediately — possible severe acute malnutrition.
items.source: WHO IMCI Chart Booklet — malnutrition assessment
